title: Charcoal grill system and method for managing temperature within the same
abstract: A method for managing temperature within a charcoal grill system includes: receiving a target grill temperature for a cooking chamber within the charcoal grill system; in response to receiving an ignition trigger, entering an ignition mode; during the ignition mode, activating an igniter within the charcoal grill system to ignite a charcoal cartridge arranged in a cartridge chamber within the charcoal grill system and activating a stoke fan coupled to the cartridge chamber to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; monitoring a temperature inside the charcoal grill system; in response to the temperature inside the charcoal grill system exceeding an ignition temperature, entering a temperature management mode; and during the temperature management mode, selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  I claim: 1. A method for managing temperature within a charcoal grill system comprising: â¢ receiving a target grill temperature for a cooking chamber within the charcoal grill system; â¢ in response to receiving an ignition trigger from a user, entering an ignition mode; â¢ during the ignition mode: o activating an igniter within the charcoal grill system to ignite a charcoal cartridge arranged in a cartridge chamber within the charcoal grill system; and o activating a stoke fan coupled to the cartridge chamber to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; â¢ monitoring a temperature inside the charcoal grill system; â¢ in response to the temperature inside the charcoal grill system exceeding an ignition temperature: o deactivating the stoke fan; o activating a cooling fan coupled to the cooking chamber to cool the cooking chamber down to the target grill temperature; and o entering a temperature management mode; and â¢ during the temperature management mode: o selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature. 2. The method of Claim l: â¢ wherein monitoring the temperature inside the charcoal grill system comprises, during the ignition mode, monitoring the temperature proximal the cartridge chamber following activation of the igniter; and â¢ wherein activating the stoke fan comprises increasing a speed of the stoke fan proportional to the temperature proximal the cartridge chamber during the ignition mode. 3. The method of Claim 1: 

â¢ wherein monitoring the temperature inside the charcoal grill system comprises monitoring the temperature proximal the cartridge chamber following activation of the igniter; â¢ further comprising deactivating the igniter in response to detecting an increase in temperature proximal the cartridge chamber following the first time; and â¢ wherein entering the temperature management mode comprises: o accessing the ignition temperature that represents complete combustion of an igniter layer in the charcoal cartridge and exceeds the target grill temperature; and o entering the temperature management mode in response to the temperature inside the charcoal grill system exceeding the ignition temperature. 4. The method of Claim 1, wherein entering the temperature management mode comprises: o at a first time succeeding the activation of the igniter, calculating a first rate of temperature change within the charcoal grill system; o confirming ignition of the charcoal cartridge in response to the first rate of temperature change exceeding a first threshold rate; o at a second time succeeding the first time, calculating a second rate of temperature change within the charcoal grill system o in response to the second rate of change falling below a second threshold rate of change, determining that the charcoal cartridge has approximately reached a peak temperature; and o entering the temperature management mode in response to determining that the charcoal cartridge has reached the ignition temperature comprising the peak temperature. 5. The method of Claim 1: â¢ further comprising: o identifying a type of the charcoal cartridge; o accessing an operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge based on the type of the charcoal cartridge; and o prompting the user to select the target grill temperature within the operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge; and 

â¢ wherein receiving the target grill temperature for the cooking chamber comprises receiving the target grill temperature, within the operating temperature range, selected by the user at a control panel coupled to the charcoal grill system. 6. The method of Claim 5: â¢ wherein identifying the type of the charcoal cartridge comprises, at the charcoal grill system, reading a unique identifier from an identification tag associated with the charcoal cartridge; â¢ wherein accessing the operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge comprises querying a lookup table stored in local memory at the charcoal grill system for the operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge based on the unique identifier, the operating temperature range defined by a minimum temperature and a maximum temperature in the cooking chamber supported by the charcoal cartridge; and â¢ wherein prompting the user to select the target grill temperature within the operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge comprises: o rendering the minimum temperature and the maximum temperature at the control panel integrated into the charcoal grill system; and o prompting the user to select the target grill temperature between the minimum temperature and the maximum temperature, inclusive, at the control panel. 7. The method of Claim 5: â¢ wherein identifying the type of the charcoal cartridge comprises receiving selection of the type of the charcoal cartridge from the control panel executing on a mobile computing device in wireless communication with the charcoal grill system; â¢ wherein receiving the target grill temperature for the cooking chamber comprises receiving the target grill temperature for the cooking chamber from the mobile computing device; and â¢ further comprising: o identifying a peak temperature inside the charcoal grill system during the ignition mode; and o confirming the type of the charcoal cartridge selected at the control panel based on the peak temperature inside the charcoal grill system during the 

 ignition mode approaching a known peak temperature associated with the type of the charcoal cartridge, in a set of known types of charcoal cartridge. 8. The method of Claim 5: â¢ wherein monitoring the temperature inside the charcoal grill system comprises monitoring the temperature inside the charcoal grill system during the ignition mode; â¢ wherein identifying the type of the charcoal cartridge comprises: o characterizing a combustion rate of the charcoal cartridge based on the temperature inside the charcoal grill system during the ignition mode; o matching the combustion rate of the charcoal cartridge during the ignition mode to a known combustion rate of a particular charcoal cartridge type in a set of charcoal cartridge types; and o identifying the charcoal cartridge as of the particular charcoal cartridge type; â¢ wherein accessing the operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge comprises retrieve the operating temperature range for the particular charcoal cartridge type. 9. The method of Claim 5: â¢ further comprising o accessing a combustion model of the charcoal cartridge based on the type of the charcoal cartridge, the combustion model estimating a combustion duration of the charcoal cartridge as a function of temperature inside the charcoal grill system; o estimating a maximum cook duration based on the combustion model and the target grill temperature selected by the user via the control panel; and o receiving a target cook duration, less than the maximum cook duration, selected by the user via the control panel; and â¢ wherein selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature during the temperature management mode comprises selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature for the target cook duration after entering the temperature management mode. 10. The method of Claim 9, further comprising: â¢ in response to expiration of the target cook duration after entering the temperature management mode, entering an active burnout mode; â¢ in the active burnout mode: o activating the stoke fan to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; and o activating the cooling fan to cool the cooking chamber; â¢ in response to the temperature inside the charcoal grill system falling below a burnout temperature: o deactivating the stoke fan to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; o deactivating the cooling fan to cool the cooking chamber; and o entering an inactive mode. 11. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ entering a cleaning mode in response to receiving a command to enter the cleaning mode from the user via the control panel during the temperature management mode; â¢ during the cleaning mode: o activating the stoke fan to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; and o deactivating the cooling fan; o in response to temperatures within the cooking chamber exceeding a threshold cleaning temperature for a threshold cleaning time, activating the cooling fan to cool the cooking chamber. 12. The method of Claim 1, further comprising, during the temperature management mode: â¢ in response to failure to increase temperature within the charcoal grill system to the target grill temperature when the stoke fan is active and the cooling fan is inactive, detecting insufficient fuel remaining in the charcoal cartridge; and â¢ in response to detecting insufficient fuel remaining in the charcoal cartridge, issuing a notification to insert a second charcoal cartridge into the cartridge chamber. 13. The method of Claim 1, further comprising: â¢ receiving a target food temperature selected by the user via the control panel; 

â¢ monitoring a temperature of a foodstuff inside the charcoal grill system via a temperature probe inserted into the foodstuff; and â¢ in response to the temperature of the foodstuff reaching the target food temperature: o deactivating the stoke fan; and o activating the cooling fan to cool the cooking chamber below the target grill temperature. 14. The method of Claim 13, further comprising: â¢ in response to the temperature of the foodstuff reaching the target food temperature: o transitioning to a low-temperature hold mode; and o serving a notification to the user to remove the foodstuff from the charcoal grill system; â¢ during the low-temperature hold mode, selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately a low hold temperature less than the target grill temperature; â¢ in response to receiving a second target grill temperature, transitioning from the low-temperature hold mode back to a second instance of the temperature management mode; and â¢ during the second instance of the temperature management mode, selectively activating the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the second target grill temperature. 15. A charcoal grill system comprising: â¢ a grill housing comprising: o a cartridge chamber configured to receive a charcoal cartridge comprising a mass of charcoal and starter material; o a cooking chamber thermally coupled to the charcoal cartridge and configured to support foodstuffs; â¢ an igniter extending into the cartridge chamber configured to contact the charcoal cartridge in the cartridge chamber; â¢ a stoke fan configured to pump ambient air into the cartridge chamber; â¢ a cooling fan configured to pump ambient air into the cooking chamber to cool the cooking chamber; â¢ a temperature sensor thermally coupled to the cooking chamber; and 

â¢ a controller configured to: o receive a target grill temperature for the cooking chamber within the charcoal grill system; o enter an ignition mode in response to receiving an ignition trigger; o during the ignition mode: â activate the igniter to ignite the charcoal cartridge within the cartridge chamber; and â activate the stoke fan to accelerate combustion of the charcoal cartridge; and o during a temperature management mode succeeding the ignition mode: â selectively activate the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature based on outputs of the temperature sensor. 16. The charcoal grill system of Claim 15: â¢ wherein the cartridge chamber is configured to receive the charcoal cartridge comprising: o a unitary charcoal briquette slab; o a combustible igniter layer extending across the unitary charcoal briquette slab; and o combustible packaging encompassing the unitary charcoal briquette slab and the combustible igniter layer; and â¢ wherein the igniter comprises a resistance heater configured: o to contact the combustible packaging of the charcoal cartridge when the charcoal cartridge is loaded in the cartridge chamber; and o to ignite the combustible packaging and the igniter layer when activated by the controller. 17. The charcoal grill system of Claim 15: â¢ further comprising a control panel coupled to the grill housing; â¢ wherein the controller is further configured: o to identify a type of the charcoal cartridge loaded into the cartridge chamber; o to access an operating temperature range of the charcoal cartridge from a database based on the type of the charcoal cartridge, the operating 

 temperature range defined by a minimum temperature and a maximum temperature in the cooking chamber supported by the charcoal cartridge; and o to update the control panel to render the minimum temperature and the maximum temperature; and o to prompt a user to select the target grill temperature between the minimum temperature and the maximum temperature at the control panel. 18. The charcoal grill system of Claim 17: â¢ further comprising a wireless scanner coupled to the control panel and configured to read a unique identifier from an identification tag associated with the charcoal cartridge; â¢ wherein the controller: o is configured to identify the type of the charcoal cartridge loaded into the cartridge chamber based on the unique identifier; and o is further configured to: â access a combustion model of the charcoal cartridge based on the type of the charcoal cartridge, the combustion model estimating a combustion duration of the charcoal cartridge as a function of temperature inside the charcoal grill system; â estimate a maximum cook duration based on the target grill temperature selected at the control panel; â update the control panel to render the maximum cook duration; â prompt the user to select a target cook duration, less than the maximum cook duration, at the control panel; and â selectively activate the stoke fan and the cooling fan to maintain the cooking chamber at approximately the target grill temperature for the target cook duration during the temperature management mode. 19. The charcoal grill system of Claim 15: â¢ further comprising a control panel coupled to the grill housing; â¢ further comprising a temperature probe coupled to the controller and configured for insertion into a foodstuff placed in the cooking chamber; â¢ wherein the controller is further configured to: 

 o receive a target food temperature selected by a user via the control panel; o monitor a temperature of the foodstuff via the temperature probe inserted into the foodstuff; and o in response to the temperature of the foodstuff reaching the target food temperature: â deactivate the stoke fan; and â activate the cooling fan to cool the cooking chamber below the target grill temperature. 20. The charcoal grill system of Claim 15: â¢ further comprising a second temperature sensor thermally coupled to the cartridge chamber; and â¢ wherein the controller is further configured to: o deactivate the igniter during the during the ignition mode in response to increase in temperature in the cartridge chamber read from the second temperature sensor; o increase a speed of the stoke fan during the ignition mode as a function of temperature in the cartridge chamber read from the second temperature sensor; o determine that the charcoal cartridge has approximately reached a peak temperature during the ignition mode in response to a rate of change in temperature in the cartridge chamber, read from the second temperature sensor, falling below a threshold rate of change; and o enter the temperature management mode in response to determining that the charcoal cartridge has reached the peak temperature.
